title: Constructors Should Only Call Non-Overridable Methods
description: This rule warns when a constructor calls an overridable method. This practice could result in failures or strange behaviors when instantiating a subclass which overrides the method. If the behavior of the child class method depends on fields that are initialized in the child class constructor, unexpected behavior can occur.
recommendation: To fix this issue, ensure that constructors only call non-overridable methods.
impact: Ignoring this rule may lead to unexpected behavior such as NullPointerExceptions due to the order of constructor calls and field initialization.

checks.message: Rule: Constructors Should Only Call Non-Overridable Methods
